binaries.title: ccffilter.sys (Cluster Shared Volume minifilter) - ECP use-after-free / dangling-pointer lifetime bug in IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-operation callback

changed_functions.name: CCFPreCreate (sub_1c0007760)
key_changes: On the CCFAddEcpToCreate success path, the UNPATCHED CCFPreCreate clears the local ECP pointer (mov qword [rsp+0x48], r14) only inside the 'if (var_b8 != 0)' branch, which is never taken because CCFAddEcpToCreate (sub_1c00090a8) zeroes var_b8. So after a successful FltInsertExtraCreateParameter the local EcpContext stays live. If a subsequent version/CSV ECP step fails (status < 0), the function's error-cleanup at 0x1c0007d5a calls FltFreeExtraCreateParameter(filter, EcpContext) on the ECP that is still linked in the IRP's ECP list. The PATCHED build adds a success-path clear of EcpContext gated by a Windows Feature Staging flag: new helper EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c00017b8) calls EvaluateFeature (sub_1c0001740) on g_Feature_2963746106_60258290 and returns (cachedstate != 1). The clear is applied via neg eax; sbb rcx,rcx; and rcx,[rsp+0x48] at the first site and a second EvaluateCurrentState-gated cmovnz at 0x1c0007c6b, so the error-cleanup free (relocated to 0x1c0007d87) sees EcpContext == NULL after a successful insert. The FltFreeExtraCreateParameter calls themselves are identical on both sides; only the local-pointer clear placement changed.

CCFAddEcpToCreate (sub_1c00090a8) obtains the IRP's ECP list via FltGetEcpListFromCallbackData (allocating a new list with FltAllocateExtraCreateParameterList + FltSetEcpListIntoCallbackData if none exists) and calls FltInsertExtraCreateParameter, i.e. it INSERTS the freshly-allocated ECP into the IRP's ECP list and transfers ownership to the IRP. It also zeroes its byref out-flag (var_b8). The pre-patch CCFPreCreate then leaves that same ECP pointer live and its error-cleanup frees it via FltFreeExtraCreateParameter while the IRP's ECP list still references it.

All other differences in this function are cosmetic: symbol stripping (sub_1c000XXXX vs named), stack-slot renumbering, moved globals (CCFCacheGuardedMutex 0x1c0004160->0x1c0004180, CCFAppInstancesCache 0x1c00040a0->0x1c00040c0, WPP control string 0x1c00032f0->0x1c0003400), and shifted branch labels (0x1c0007c61->0x1c0007c85, 0x1c0007d43->0x1c0007d70). The CSV/version-ECP sub-flow (CCFFindCsvSetHandlePropertiesEcp / CCFLookupAndAddVersionEcpToCreate) is structurally identical on both sides.
changed_functions.security_notes: ECP lifetime/ownership flaw in the IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-operation callback of the CSV minifilter. CCFPreCreate allocates a GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE Extra Create Parameter with FltAllocateExtraCreateParameter, hands it to CCFAddEcpToCreate which inserts it into the create IRP's ECP list (FltInsertExtraCreateParameter => the filter manager now owns/tracks the ECP for the lifetime of the IRP) and zeroes its out-flag var_b8. On the success path the pre-patch CCFPreCreate does not clear its local copy of the ECP pointer (the clear sits inside a never-taken 'if (var_b8)' branch), so it stays live through the version/CSV ECP sub-flow. If that sub-flow returns an error, the function's error-cleanup calls FltFreeExtraCreateParameter on the same pointer (0x1c0007d5a) while it is still linked in the IRP's ECP list, leaving a dangling list entry; when the create completes (or another filter / the manager walks the ECP list) the freed pool is dereferenced => kernel pool use-after-free / pool corruption. The patch clears the local ECP pointer on the success path, gated by a runtime Feature Staging check (EvaluateCurrentState / sub_1c00017b8 reading g_Feature_2963746106_60258290_cachedstate == 1), so the error-cleanup can no longer free the ECP FltMgr already owns. Reachable from any user-mode process that opens a file on a CSV-backed volume with an EA buffer that drives the app-instance ECP allocation path (parsed by CCFFilterAppInstanceEA).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED vulnerable region (CCFPreCreate error-cleanup) ===
; edi = status (< 0 after a failed version/CSV ECP step); [rsp+0x48] = EcpContext (still in IRP ECP list), arg2[1] = filter handle
test    edi, edi
jns     0x1c0007d79
mov     rdx, qword [rsp+0x48]       ; EcpContext (still listed)
test    rdx, rdx
jz      0x1c0007d6b
mov     rax, qword [rsp+0x58]
mov     rcx, qword [rax+0x8]
call    cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter  ; 0x1c0007d5a -- frees still-listed ECP => UAF
mov     qword [rsp+0x48], r14

=== PATCHED success path (adds local-pointer clear before the cleanup can fire) ===
call    cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter  ; consumed branch (unchanged, unreached)
call    EvaluateCurrentState        ; returns (cachedstate != 1)
neg     eax                         ; mask: 0 if flag==1, -1 if flag!=1
sbb     rcx, rcx
and     rcx, qword [rsp+0x48]
mov     qword [rsp+0x48], rcx
... (0x1c0007c6b) ...
call    EvaluateCurrentState        ; success-path clear (runs regardless of var_b8)
mov     rcx, qword [rsp+0x48]
test    eax, eax
cmovnz  rcx, r14                    ; if (flag!=1) EcpContext = nullptr
mov     qword [rsp+0x48], rcx
; error-cleanup free relocated to 0x1c0007d87, now sees EcpContext == NULL after a successful insert

=== EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c00017b8, patch-only helper @ 0x1c00017b8) ===
sub     rsp, 0x28
lea     rcx, g_Feature_2963746106_60258290_FeatureDescriptorDetails
call    EvaluateFeature             ; sub_1c0001740
mov     rax, cs:g_Feature_2963746106_60258290_FeatureDescriptorDetails
mov     ecx, dword [rax]            ; cachedstate
xor     eax, eax
cmp     ecx, 0x1
setnz   al                          ; return (cachedstate != 1)
add     rsp, 0x28
retn

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Entire diff is symbol/label stripping and inter-build address shifts: CCFDispatchUnsupported (sub_1c00081f0), CCFDispatchDeviceControl (sub_1c0007f30), CCFProcessNotification (sub_1c0008270), CCFApplyDeviceAcl (sub_1c00096d8), CCFUnload (sub_1c0007590), WPP_SF_d (sub_1c0001034), etc. Global addresses shifted (data_1c00041a8->0x1c00041c8, CCFCacheGuardedMutex->0x1c0004180). Logic (FltRegisterFilter, IoCreateDevice, PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine, FltStartFiltering, device-ACL application, unload-on-failure) is equivalent.

Confirmed: device is \Device\CCFFilter created with IoCreateDevice(DeviceType=0x14, Characteristics=0x100) and a restrictive ACL applied (CCFApplyDeviceAcl / sub_1c00096d8). IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL dispatch (CCFDispatchDeviceControl / sub_1c0007f30) handles IOCTL codes 0x140000/0x14400c/0x144018/0x144004/0x144008 with explicit InputBufferLength/OutputBufferLength size checks (cmp r9d, 0x20/0x28/0x10/0x8). No behavioral change vs unpatched.

security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-operation callback (FltMgr minifilter) — triggered by NtCreateFile/CreateFile on a Cluster Shared Volume (CSV) backed path
call_chain: NtCreateFile / CreateFileW on a CSV-mounted path (user mode)
CCFPreCreate (sub_1c0007760) — pre-operation callback registered via FltRegisterFilter(&data_1c0003000)
CCFFilterAppInstanceEA — parses the create's EaBuffer for a CCFF app-instance EA (sets var_b7)
CCFPreCreate: CCFAddEcpToCreate (sub_1c00090a8) -> FltInsertExtraCreateParameter (ECP now owned by IRP's ECP list; var_b8 zeroed)
CCFPreCreate: version/CSV ECP step (CCFFindAppInstanceVersionEcp / CCFLookupAndAddVersionEcpToCreate) fails -> status < 0
CCFPreCreate error-cleanup: FltFreeExtraCreateParameter(EcpContext) at 0x1c0007d5a  <-- frees ECP still referenced by IRP ECP list => UAF
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use-After-Free / kernel pool lifetime (ECP ownership) bug (CWE-416). Class: ECP freed by the error-cleanup path while still linked into the create IRP's Extra Create Parameter list.
security_findings.description: In the pre-patch ccffilter.sys, the IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-op callback CCFPreCreate allocates a network app-instance Extra Create Parameter (GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE, 0x14 bytes, tag 'CCFF') and passes it to CCFAddEcpToCreate. CCFAddEcpToCreate (sub_1c00090a8) obtains the IRP's ECP list via FltGetEcpListFromCallbackData (allocating a new list with FltAllocateExtraCreateParameterList + FltSetEcpListIntoCallbackData if none exists), calls FltInsertExtraCreateParameter(filter, EcpList, EcpContext), and zeroes its byref out-flag var_b8. FltInsertExtraCreateParameter transfers ownership: FltMgr now tracks the ECP inside the IRP's ECP list for the remainder of the create. On the success path CCFPreCreate does NOT clear its local copy of the ECP pointer (the clear it has is inside an 'if (var_b8 != 0)' branch that is never taken, since CCFAddEcpToCreate just zeroed var_b8), so EcpContext stays live through the version/CSV ECP sub-flow. If that sub-flow (CCFFindAppInstanceVersionEcp / CCFLookupAndAddVersionEcpToCreate) returns an error, the create status goes negative and the function's error-cleanup runs 'if (status < 0 && EcpContext) FltFreeExtraCreateParameter(filter, EcpContext)' at 0x1c0007d5a. Because the ECP is still linked in the IRP's ECP list, this leaves a dangling entry. When the create IRP later completes (or when the filter manager / another minifilter walks the ECP list, e.g. via FltFindExtraCreateParameter or ECP-list teardown at IRP completion), the freed pool backing the ECP context is dereferenced, producing a kernel pool use-after-free / pool corruption. The patch adds a success-path clear of the local EcpContext pointer gated by a Windows Feature Staging flag (new helper EvaluateCurrentState / sub_1c00017b8, which calls EvaluateFeature / sub_1c0001740 on g_Feature_2963746106_60258290 and returns (cachedstate != 1)); with the feature enabled the local pointer is nulled right after a successful insert, so the error-cleanup free (relocated to 0x1c0007d87) no longer frees the ECP FltMgr already owns.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) ccffilter.sys (CSV minifilter) is loaded and filtering a Cluster Shared Volume. 2) A user-mode process issues NtCreateFile/CreateFile against a file on a CSV-backed path supplying an EaBuffer that carries a CCFF app-instance EA, so that CCFFilterAppInstanceEA(&Iopb->Parameters.Create.EaBuffer, ...) sets var_b7 and causes the GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE allocation + CCFAddEcpToCreate insert path to execute. 3) The pre-existing app-instance ECP is NOT already present (CCFFindAppInstanceEcp returns var_b8==0 at entry), so the allocate/insert sequence runs. 4) The subsequent version/CSV ECP step (CCFFindAppInstanceVersionEcp / CCFLookupAndAddVersionEcpToCreate) fails, driving the create status negative while EcpContext is still live, so execution reaches the error-cleanup FltFreeExtraCreateParameter at 0x1c0007d5a on the still-listed ECP. 5) A subsequent ECP-list operation (create completion, or a second create/FS op that queries the ECP list) touches the freed ECP => observable UAF.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel pool double-free / use-after-free. The freed ECP context is a 0x14-byte pool allocation (tag 'CCFF'). It is freed at 0x1c0007d5a while still linked in the create IRP's ECP list, so the filter manager frees the same block again (or walks it after free) when it tears down the ECP list at create completion, corrupting the kernel pool. The primitive is reached only on a secondary error path (a version/CSV ECP sub-step failing after a successful app-instance ECP insert), which is not directly attacker-controlled, so triggering is non-deterministic. No control over the freed block's replacement contents or a follow-on read/write/function-pointer primitive is demonstrable from these binaries; the reachable impact is kernel pool corruption, i.e. local denial of service (bugcheck). No privilege-escalation path is demonstrated.
security_findings.assembly:
; ===== UNPATCHED ccffilter_unpatched.sys : CCFPreCreate @ 0x1c0007760 (key ECP lifetime blocks) =====
; Block 0x1c0007baa (CCFAddEcpToCreate call site)
0x1c0007baa: call    CCFAddEcpToCreate        ; (rcx=CallbackData, rdx=EcpContext, r8=&var_b8) -> inserts ECP into IRP ECP list; var_b8=0
0x1c0007bb5: test    eax, eax
0x1c0007bb7: jns     0x1c0007bfd              ; if (CCFAddEcpToCreate >= 0) goto success path

; Block 0x1c0007bfd (never-taken consumed branch; var_b8 already 0)
0x1c0007bfd: cmp     byte [rsp+0x40], r14b     ; var_b8 (== 0)
0x1c0007c02: jz      0x1c0007c61
0x1c0007c4b: call    cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter  ; consumed-branch free (unchanged, unreached)
0x1c0007c57: mov     qword [rsp+0x48], r14     ; EcpContext = NULL (inside never-taken branch)

; Block 0x1c0007d43 (error-cleanup: THE vulnerable free)
0x1c0007d43: test    edi, edi                  ; edi = status (< 0 after a failed version/CSV ECP step)
0x1c0007d45: jns     0x1c0007d79
0x1c0007d47: mov     rdx, qword [rsp+0x48]     ; EcpContext (still in IRP ECP list)
0x1c0007d4c: test    rdx, rdx
0x1c0007d4f: jz      0x1c0007d6b
mov     rax, qword [rsp+0x58]
mov     rcx, qword [rax+0x8]                  ; filter handle
0x1c0007d5a: call    cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter  ; *** frees still-listed ECP => UAF ***
0x1c0007d66: mov     qword [rsp+0x48], r14

; ===== PATCHED ccffilter_patched.sys : success-path clear before cleanup can fire =====
0x1c0007c4b: call    cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter  ; consumed branch (unchanged)
0x1c0007c57: call    EvaluateCurrentState      ; return (cachedstate != 1)
0x1c0007c5c: neg     eax                       ; mask = (flag==1) ? 0 : -1
0x1c0007c5e: sbb     rcx, rcx
0x1c0007c61: and     rcx, qword [rsp+0x48]     ; rcx = mask & EcpContext
0x1c0007c66: mov     qword [rsp+0x48], rcx
0x1c0007c6b: call    EvaluateCurrentState      ; success-path clear
0x1c0007c70: mov     rcx, qword [rsp+0x48]
0x1c0007c75: test    eax, eax
0x1c0007c77: cmovnz  rcx, r14                  ; if (flag!=1) EcpContext = nullptr
0x1c0007c7b: mov     qword [rsp+0x48], rcx
0x1c0007c80: jmp     0x1c0007c85
; error-cleanup free relocated to 0x1c0007d87 (same guarded shape), now sees EcpContext == NULL after a successful insert

breakpoints.why: Entry to the IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-op callback. Confirm this is a CSV-path create: dump arg1->Iopb->TargetFileObjectName to verify the path is on a CSV volume. arg1 = FLT_CALLBACK_DATA (rcx), arg2 = FLT_RELATED_OBJECTS (rdx).

The FltInsertExtraCreateParameter ownership transfer happens inside CCFAddEcpToCreate. After this returns >= 0, the ECP at [rsp+0x48] is owned by the IRP's ECP list and var_b8 is 0. rcx=CallbackData, rdx=EcpContext, r8=&var_b8. Watch rdx = the ECP pointer that will later be freed while still listed.

The vulnerable free. rcx = filter handle, rdx = EcpContext (ECP STILL linked in the IRP ECP list). This block is reached only when edi (status) is negative -- i.e. a version/CSV ECP step failed while EcpContext was still live. Confirm with !fltkd or by dumping the IRP's ECP list before/after to see the dangling entry.

watch: [rsp+0x48] == EcpContext : the GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE (0x14-byte, tag 'CCFF') pool allocation
rdx at the error-cleanup FltFreeExtraCreateParameter call (0x1c0007d5a) = ECP being freed (dangling-after-free target)
rcx at the free = ccffilter filter handle passed to Flt* ECP APIs
the IRP's ECP_LIST for this create (obtain via FltGetEcpListFromCallbackData) — must still contain the freed ECP pointer post-free
edi = status; must be < 0 (failed version/CSV ECP step) to reach the vulnerable free block 0x1c0007d43
key_offsets: 0x1c0007baa : call CCFAddEcpToCreate (ownership transfer via FltInsertExtraCreateParameter; var_b8 zeroed)
0x1c0007c4b : consumed-branch call FltFreeExtraCreateParameter (unchanged, unreached because var_b8 == 0)
0x1c0007d5a : call cs:__imp_FltFreeExtraCreateParameter (THE bug - frees still-listed ECP)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Load ccffilter_unpatched.sys (CSV minifilter) on a system with a Cluster Shared Volume mounted. From a standard user process, call CreateFileW/NtCreateFile on a path under the CSV mount (e.g. C:\ClusterStorage\Volume1\<file>) with an EaBuffer containing a CCFF app-instance EA so CCFFilterAppInstanceEA takes the app-instance branch (var_b7 set) AND the existing-app-instance ECP is absent (CCFFindAppInstanceEcp sets var_b8=0 at entry), forcing the FltAllocateExtraCreateParameter -> CCFAddEcpToCreate insert sequence in label_1c0007a96. Arrange for the subsequent version/CSV ECP step to fail (for example an internal ECP allocation/insert failure; this is not directly attacker-controlled) so the create status goes negative and execution reaches the error-cleanup FltFreeExtraCreateParameter at 0x1c0007d5a on the still-listed ECP. The filter manager's ECP-list teardown at create completion then frees / walks the same block again.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the error-cleanup FltFreeExtraCreateParameter call (0x1c0007d5a) the ECP at [rsp+0x48] is freed while the IRP's ECP list still references it. When the filter manager tears down the create's ECP list at IRP_MJ_CREATE completion, the same block is freed again / walked after free, producing kernel pool corruption and a bugcheck. With Driver Verifier Special Pool enabled on the CCFF tag, the invalid free/dereference is caught at the point it occurs, with the faulting address at the freed ECP.
debugging_aids.notes: CCFPreCreate uses a GS security cookie (__security_check_cookie at exit). The IRP_MJ_CREATE path is the high-reach surface; the CCFDispatchDeviceControl IOCTL router (sub_1c0007f30, codes 0x140000/0x14400c/0x144018/0x144004/0x144008) already enforces explicit InputBufferLength/OutputBufferLength checks and is unchanged by the patch — the vulnerability is exclusively in the ECP create path. EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c00017b8, patch-only) is a Windows Feature Staging gate that reads g_Feature_2963746106_60258290_cachedstate via EvaluateFeature (sub_1c0001740); it gates only the local pointer clear, not whether the ECP is inserted — so on the unpatched build the still-listed ECP is freed by the error-cleanup whenever a version/CSV ECP step fails.

summary: ccffilter.sys is the Cluster Shared Volume (CSV) FltMgr minifilter. The patch modifies exactly one function with real behavior: CCFPreCreate (sub_1c0007760), the IRP_MJ_CREATE pre-operation callback (DriverEntry is a cosmetic symbol/address strip). The pre-patch flaw is an Extra Create Parameter (ECP) lifetime/ownership bug: CCFPreCreate allocates a GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE ECP, hands it to CCFAddEcpToCreate (sub_1c00090a8) which inserts it into the create IRP's ECP list via FltInsertExtraCreateParameter (transferring ownership) and zeroes its out-flag var_b8. On the success path CCFPreCreate leaves the local ECP pointer live (its clear is inside a never-taken 'if (var_b8)' branch), so if the subsequent version/CSV ECP step fails, the function's error-cleanup frees the ECP via FltFreeExtraCreateParameter (0x1c0007d5a) while the IRP's ECP list still references it — leaving a dangling ECP-list entry that is dereferenced when the create completes or the ECP list is walked, yielding a kernel pool use-after-free / pool corruption. The fix clears the local ECP pointer on the success path behind a runtime Feature Staging check (new helper EvaluateCurrentState / sub_1c00017b8 reading g_Feature_2963746106_60258290_cachedstate == 1 via EvaluateFeature / sub_1c0001740) so the error-cleanup can no longer free the ECP FltMgr already owns. A PoC needs to open (CreateFile/NtCreateFile) a file on a CSV-backed path with an EA buffer that drives the app-instance ECP allocation+insert path and forces the version/CSV ECP step to fail, then rely on the filter manager's ECP-list teardown to free/walk the block a second time. The fix is delivered behind a Windows Feature Staging runtime flag (staged rollout), so the patched binary retains the unpatched behavior when the feature is not active. The secondary ECP-step failure is not directly attacker-controlled, and the reachable impact is kernel pool corruption / local denial of service; no privilege-escalation path is demonstrable from these binaries.
